source.description: Six-panel raster marker contact sheet generated for the June 14, 2026 retro arcade joystick sticker archive lesson.
source.notes: Promoted after the owner preferred this generated game-controller direction over the cartoon party balloon backfill.

process_strategy: The sequence establishes the sticker base, joystick stem, ball top, buttons, thick outline, face, star, color fills, and shadow before the final panel only reinforces existing ink, fills, and highlights.

final_elements.name: rounded sticker base
final_elements.description: A rounded rectangle arcade base tilted slightly on the page.

final_elements.name: joystick stem
final_elements.description: A short stem rising from the base.

final_elements.name: ball top
final_elements.description: A round ball at the top of the joystick stem.

final_elements.name: button circles
final_elements.description: Two simple arcade buttons on the base.

final_elements.name: thick black outline
final_elements.description: A bold marker outline around the base, joystick, and buttons.

final_elements.name: face and small star accent
final_elements.description: A simple face on the base plus one small star accent.

final_elements.name: teal base and red ball fill
final_elements.description: Marker fill inside the already drawn base and ball top.

final_elements.name: yellow buttons and gray shadow
final_elements.description: Marker fill on the existing buttons plus an offset sticker shadow.

final_elements.name: small white highlights
final_elements.description: Small shine marks on the joystick ball, buttons, and base.

frames.visible_job: Places the rounded sticker base guide and joystick stem.
Adds the joystick ball and two button circles to the existing base.
Thickens only the existing base, joystick, and button edges into a bold black outline.
Adds a small face on the base and one star accent near the joystick.
Fills the established base, ball, and buttons with marker color and adds an offset shadow and small highlights.
introduces: teal base and red ball fill

final_step.visible_job: Retraces existing outlines, deepens marker fills, and clarifies the face, star, shadow, and highlights already placed.

rejection_checks: No base, joystick stem, ball, button, face, star, color area, shadow, or highlight should first appear in the final image.
No frame may ink or color a shape that was not already visible earlier.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same joystick pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
